cards.title: Cut manual work by ~95% with automation
cards.description: Replaced Handshake AI's manual annotation pipeline with [AutoHandshake](#project-autohandshake) — reliable Selenium automation that runs unattended, recovers from failures, and keeps a full audit trail. Recognized with Handshake's [Model Validation 2 — Expert](#certs-section) credential.

cards.title: Built a developer-ready AI integration library
cards.description: [Intuit](#project-intuit) lets applications plug into OpenAI, Claude, and local language models through one clean interface — streaming chat, embeddings, and retrieval fast enough to run on a laptop. My go-to foundation for building AI-powered tools.

cards.title: Shipped a full trading-platform SDK
cards.description: Reverse-engineered Webull's trading platform into [Webull-SDK](#project-webull-sdk) — real-time stock, options, and crypto market data with secure authentication, ready for algorithmic strategies and dashboards without wrestling the wire format.

cards.title: Unified five scientific databases behind one API
cards.description: [Akashi](#project-akashi) pulls PubChem, Wikipedia, PsychonautWiki, PubMed, and PMC into one developer-friendly interface — the retrieval backbone for source-grounded AI answers and [Chemica](#project-chemica). Full story in [Building Akashi](/articles/chemica-api).

cards.title: Low-level .NET runtime engineering
cards.description: Built [Cassowary](#project-cassowary) (C#) and its D successor [Godwit](#project-godwit) to reach into .NET's internals — advanced reflection, allocation, and inspection that most libraries can't touch. See [Hacking C#](/articles/syscall-hooks) for syscall-level security work.

cards.title: Credentialed in AI, security, and data
cards.description: Microsoft Career Essentials in [Generative AI, Azure AI, Software Development, and Security](#certs-section), plus SQL Programming, KNIME Data Science, GitHub essentials, and CompTIA full-stack web — the credentials behind every [skill](#skills) I ship with.
